content: Bosch Washer Error CodesBelow is a complete list of Bosch Washer error codes, what each code means, and the issues they typically indicate.Error CodeDescriptionCLChild Lock is activated and controls are disabled.Drain IconThe washer detected slow or restricted draining.Drum Clean IconThe washer is reminding you to run a Drum Clean cycle.E00 / -18The drain pump is blocked or unable to rotate.E00 / -23Water has collected in the base pan, triggering the leak sensor.E10The i-DOS detergent dosing pump is blocked or restricted.E16The washer door is not locked or cannot lock.E17The washer is not filling with water fast enough.E18The washer is draining too slowly or not at all.E19The washer is unable to heat water properly.E20The washer detected abnormal temperature readings.E23The AquaStop leak protection system has detected water.E25The turbidity sensor is reporting invalid or no readings.E26The flow sensor is reporting abnormal or no readings.E28The washer detected abnormal flow meter readings.Er:01The washer door is open or not detected as closed.Er:02The washer cannot release the door lock.Er:03The washer cannot lock the door.Er:04The door lock mechanism is defective.Er:05The temperature sensor (NTC) is shorted.Er:06The temperature sensor (NTC) is open or disconnected.Er:07The washer detected unexpected heating activity.Er:09The motor cannot accelerate properly.Er:10The motor is not rotating when commanded.Er:11The motor reversing relay test failed.Er:12The washer detected a water inlet fault.Er:13The washer detected a drain pump malfunction.Er:14The washer took too long to heat the water.Er:15The heating element is reporting a fault.Er:16The washer detected an excessively high water level.Er:17The washer detected insufficient water level.Er:18The AquaStop leak protection system has been activated.Er:19The pressure sensor is reporting invalid or inconsistent water level readings.Er:20The pressure switch is not responding correctly.Er:21The washer detected excessive foam during the cycle.Er:22The washer aborted the spin cycle due to imbalance or safety conditions.Er:23The washer detected a software or control module error.F27The pressure sensor is reporting invalid water level readings.Key IconThe washer door is locked for safety.Pause/Play FlashingThe washer cycle is paused or waiting for user input.Suds IconThe washer detected excessive foam inside the drum.Tap IconThe washer detected a water supply issue.
summary: A complete reference guide to Bosch washer error codes and what they mean.
title: Bosch Washer Error Codes

content: Electrolux Washer Error CodesBelow is a complete list of Electrolux Washer error codes, what each code means, and the issues they typically indicate.Error CodeDescription01EThe washer detected insufficient water flow during filling.02EThe washer detected a heating system malfunction.03EThe washer detected a drain system malfunction.07EThe washer detected a temperature sensor (NTC) fault.1The washer did not detect water entering the machine.10The washer detected that the motor is not running when commanded.10EThe washer detected a motor system malfunction.11The motor failed to reach the programmed speed.12The motor exceeded the safe speed limit.13The washer detected a tachometer signal fault.14The washer detected motor overheating.15The washer detected that the motor is stalled.16The washer detected irregular drum rotation.17The washer detected an unbalanced load.18The washer did not drain within the expected time.19The washer detected a water level sensor fault.2The washer detected that the door is open.20The washer detected low water level during heating.21The washer detected a heating element malfunction.22The washer detected an out-of-range temperature sensor reading.23The washer detected a heating relay malfunction.24The washer's heating safety cutoff has been triggered.25The washer detected excessive water level during heating.26The washer detected an unexpected drop in water level.27The washer detected a general overfill condition.28The washer detected a general underfill condition.29The washer detected a calibration error in the water level sensor.3The washer cannot lock the door.30The washer detected an electrical fault in the water level sensor circuit.31The washer detected a communication fault between control boards.32The washer detected a memory fault in the control board.33The washer detected an invalid or missing configuration.34The washer cannot communicate with the motor inverter.35The washer detected an inverter malfunction.36The washer detected a missing or incorrect motor phase.37The washer detected excessive motor current.38The washer detected insufficient motor current.39The washer detected invalid motor speed sensor feedback.4The washer cannot unlock the door.40The washer detected an overvoltage condition.41The washer detected an undervoltage condition.42The washer detected a phase imbalance or missing phase.43The washer detected an internal control board malfunction.5The washer detected an excessively high water level.6The washer did not reach the programmed water level.7The washer failed to heat water within the expected time.8The washer detected overheating.9The washer detected a motor overload condition.E10The washer is not receiving water at the expected rate.E11The washer did not fill with water during the wash phase.E12The washer did not fill with water during the rinse phase.E13The washer detected water loss during the cycle.E20The washer is draining too slowly or not at all.E21The washer detected slow or incomplete draining.E22The washer did not drain after the rinse cycle.E23The control board detected a drain pump triac sensing fault.E24The control board detected a drain pump triac circuit failure.E25The control board received an invalid or unexpected signal.E31The pressure sensor is reporting invalid or unstable water level readings.E32The pressure sensor calibration is incorrect or out of range.E35The washer detected an excessively high water level.E40The washer detected a general door lock system fault.E41The washer detected that the door is not closed.E42The washer cannot lock the door.E43The control board detected a door lock triac fault.E44The washer is not receiving proper feedback from the door lock.E45The control board’s door lock circuit has failed.E50The washer detected a general motor system fault.E60The washer detected a general heating system fault.E61The washer failed to heat water to the required temperature.E62The washer detected that water temperature is too high.EC1The washer received an incorrect signal from the flow meter.EF1The washer detected a drain pump or filter blockage.EF2The washer detected excessive foam inside the drum.EF4The flow meter is not detecting water flow.EF5The washer detected an out-of-balance condition.EH0The washer detected an electrical supply irregularity.
summary: A complete reference guide to Electrolux washer error codes and what they mean.
title: Electrolux Washer Error Codes

content:
Welcome to Error Code Hub # Error Code Hub is a growing library of appliance, TV, computer, and phone error codes, organized so you can quickly find:
what the error code means the symptoms you may notice the most common causes step‑by‑step fixes safety notes applicable models Whether you’re troubleshooting a washer, diagnosing a refrigerator, or trying to understand a TV or laptop error, this site gives you clear, practical explanations without the confusing technical jargon.
Browse by Device Type # Start by choosing the type of device you’re troubleshooting:
Appliances TVs Computers Phones Other Devices Popular Brands # Explore error codes for major brands:
Whirlpool Samsung LG GE Bosch Sony Vizio (Add or remove brands as your content grows.)
Latest Additions # Here are some of the newest pages added to the site:
Check the Devices section for the most recently added error codes:
👉 View All Devices
Why This Site Exists # Error codes can be confusing — especially when manuals are vague or missing. Error Code Hub was created to provide:
clear explanations of what each code means real‑world symptoms you might see common causes based on technician experience safe, practical fixes anyone can follow model‑specific notes when brands reuse codes This helps you diagnose problems faster and decide whether you can fix the issue yourself or need a technician.
How to Use This Site # Search for your brand + device + error code Read the meaning and symptoms Follow the step‑by‑step fixes Review safety notes before attempting repairs Check the applicable models list to confirm accuracy Disclaimer # This site is for informational purposes only. Electrical and mechanical repairs involve risk. Always follow manufacturer instructions and consider contacting a qualified technician for diagnosis or repair.
summary: A complete database of appliance, TV, computer, and phone error codes — organized by brand, device type, and model.
title: Error Code Hub

content:
Welcome to Minted Tools # Minted Tools is a collection of free, practical, no‑nonsense tools designed to help people solve real‑world problems. Each project is built to be fast, lightweight, and easy to use — with no sign‑ups, no paywalls, and no clutter.
Our goal is simple:
Create tools that save time, reduce frustration, and deliver clear answers.
Featured Project: Error Code Hub # Error Code Hub →
Error Code Hub is a growing database of appliance, TV, computer, and phone error codes, organized by:
device type brand model error code Each page includes:
what the error code means symptoms you may notice common causes step‑by‑step fixes safety notes applicable models If you’re troubleshooting a washer, diagnosing a refrigerator, or trying to understand a TV or laptop error, Error Code Hub gives you clear explanations without the confusing technical jargon.
What Minted Tools Offers # Minted Tools is built around three core principles:
1. Practical Tools # Every project solves a real problem — whether it’s troubleshooting, diagnostics, organization, or automation.
2. Clean, Fast, Lightweight Design # No ads (unless required for monetization), no bloat, no distractions.
Just fast, useful tools that load instantly.
3. Built to Scale # Each tool is designed to grow over time, with new features, categories, and improvements added regularly.
Current Projects # 🔧 Error Code Hub # A complete database of appliance, TV, computer, and phone error codes.
👉 Visit: Error Code Hub
Upcoming Tools # Minted Tools will expand with additional utilities, including:
troubleshooting assistants diagnostic checklists home maintenance tools tech reference guides calculators and workflow helpers As new tools launch, they will appear here on the homepage.
About Minted Tools # Minted Tools is built with:
Hugo for speed Blowfish for clean design GitHub Pages for reliability modular architecture for easy expansion Every project is designed to be:
fast mobile‑friendly accessible SEO‑optimized easy to maintain Stay Updated # New tools and updates are added regularly.
Bookmark this page and check back as the Minted Tools ecosystem grows.
summary: Minted Tools is a hub for practical, free tools and resources — starting with Error Code Hub.
title: Minted Tools

content: Samsung Washer Error CodesBelow is a complete list of Samsung Washer error codes, what each code means, and the issues they typically indicate.Error CodeDescription0Washer remains on after the cycle ends.1CWater level sensor malfunction.3CMotor operation issue detected.3CPElectronic control system needs to be checked.4CWater is not supplied.4C2Cold water hose is connected to the hot water tap.8CMEMS vibration sensor malfunction.8C1MEMS vibration sensor malfunction (variant 1).8C2MEMS vibration sensor malfunction (variant 2).9C1Electronic control system check required.9C2Electronic control system malfunction.ACCommunication error between main and sub control boards.AC6Communication error between main PCB and inverter PCB.C5The washer is taking too long to drain.dCDoor is not properly closed.DC1Main door lock/unlock mechanism malfunction.HCWasher detects excessively hot water.LCWasher detects a drain hose issue.LC1Washer detects a drain hose blockage or improper placement.OcWasher detects water overflow.SFSystem failure detected.UbUnbalanced load detected during spin.UCWasher detects unstable or insufficient power.
summary: A complete reference guide to Samsung washer error codes and what they mean.
title: Samsung Washer Error Codes

content: Whirlpool Washer Error CodesBelow is a complete list of Whirlpool Washer error codes, what each code means, and the issues they typically indicate.Error CodeDescriptiondETDetergent cartridge not detected.F0 E1Load detected during the Clean Washer cycle.F0 E2Oversuds condition detected.F0 E3Overload detected.F0 E5Off‑balance load detected.F06Motor tachometer error.F07Motor control unit (MCU) failure.F09Overflow condition detected.F1Primary control board failure.F1 E1Main control board failure.F11Serial communication error between CCU and MCU.F14EEPROM or memory error.F15Motor control unit overheating.F2Stuck key or user interface failure.F22Door lock failure.F23Heater failure detected.F24Water temperature sensor error.F25Drive motor thermal protector tripped.F26Door switch failure detected.F27Overflow or pressure switch stuck on 'full'.F28Communication error between CCU and MCU.F29Door unlock failure.F3 E1Water level system error detected.F3 E2Temperature sensor (NTC) error.F30Dispenser system error.F31Pressure switch failure detected.F32Pressure switch failure detected.F33Pump drive system error.F34Water level out of expected range.F35Analog pressure sensor failure.F36Heater relay failure.F37Temperature sensor failure detected.F40Water level or pressure system error.F41Motor control failure detected.F42Motor overcurrent detected.F43Motor stalled during operation.F48Water temperature out of expected range.F5Lid switch or lid lock fault.F5 E2Door lock error detected.F5 E3Door unlock failure.F51Rotor position sensor failure or out-of-range signal.F52Motor overload detected.F54Motor overheating detected.F6 E3Communication error between UI and main control.F7Motor unable to start or run.F7 E1Motor speed sensing error.F7 E7Motor unable to reach target RPM.F72Basket re-engagement failure.F73Basket disengagement failure.F8Water supply or fill error.F8 E1No water detected entering the washer.F8 E3Overflow condition detected.F83Water level calibration error.F84Water level sensor reading out of range.F9Washer is taking too long to drain.F9 E1Long drain time detected.F99System fault or control board error.LO FLLow water flow or no water entering the washer.LOCControl Lock is activated.SUDExcessive suds detected in the washer.SUDSExcessive suds detected in the washer.
summary: A complete reference guide to Whirlpool washer error codes and what they mean.
